# Polaris Documentation

> User and developer documentation for Polaris Finance — the self-scaling stablecoin operating system. Mint pAssets backed entirely by onchain collateral and yield. No T-bills. No CEXs. No compromises.

## Polaris Finance Documentation

URL: https://docs.polarisfinance.io/

# Polaris Finance Documentation

Polaris is the **pETH-powered yield layer for all of DeFi**, a self-scaling stablecoin operating system. It produces yield-bearing **pETH** and uses it as the base collateral for minting synthetic assets (**pAssets**) through onchain debt positions.

The infrastructure is trustless and immutable, and every pAsset is backed entirely by onchain collateral and yield, with no T-bill or CEX dependency. The result is a competitive, risk-adjusted yield source for both ETH and stablecoins. Through **Flows**, third-party protocols can build on Polaris assets and share in protocol yield.

This documentation explains how the system works, how to use it, and the trade-offs it makes. For the current network, contract, and actionability status, start with [Launch Status](/launch-status).

## Launch timeline

## The three engines

Polaris is three products that each stand on their own, but whose real power is their synergy. The bonding curve makes pETH work. The CDP makes pAssets work. The conversion mechanism and POLAR make them work together:

Engine | What it is | Why it matters

**1.&nbsp;pETH** | ETH deposited into a two-way **bonding curve**, with a floor price designed to rise under the curve invariants | A capital-efficient, yield-bearing collateral with a contract-defined exit path under stated assumptions
**2.&nbsp;pAssets** | Synthetic pegged (USD, Gold, etc.) assets minted against collateral, via a Collateralized Debt Position (CDP) | Counterparty-free stables that scale with onchain activity
**3.&nbsp;POLAR** | The stewardship token, minted only by burning pETH in a **conversion auction** | Aligns long-term incentives and steers the protocol within hardcoded bounds

## Timed explainers

## A note on this documentation

Polaris is a developing protocol still in testnet. This documentation reflects the current Testnet 2 state as accurately as possible.

---

## Changelog

URL: https://docs.polarisfinance.io/changelog

# Changelog

This is a changelog for **this documentation**, not the Polaris protocol. It records what changed in these pages — structure, content, and accessibility — so returning readers can see what is new.
Entries follow [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com), use ISO dates (YYYY-MM-DD), and are listed newest first.

## [2026-06-08]

A documentation best-practices pass that upgrades existing pages without changing the protocol model.

### Added

- Point-of-decision risk notes on the action pages (open a trove, redemptions, Stability Pool deposits, conversions), each linking to the full Risk Disclosure.
- A POLAR tokenomics page covering distribution, allocations, and vesting.
- The Polaris system overview diagram on Core Concepts and the lineage diagram on Why Polaris, each with descriptive alt text, a caption, and a numbered text description so the meaning survives without the image.
- Developer **Gotchas** and **Failure modes** sections, plus a worked integration code example, in the developer pages.
- An `llms.txt` machine-readable export so integrators and agents can consume the docs programmatically.
- This documentation changelog.

### Changed

- Enriched the six role paths from thin link lists into role-specific reading sequences, each with a "Who this is for" line, prerequisites, and a one-line note on why each step matters.
- Added audience signposting that routes developers and users to the right starting point.
- Accessibility pass across the affected pages, including image alternatives and text descriptions for every embedded diagram.
- Trimmed over-long meta descriptions so search snippets don't truncate.
- Consolidated repeated cross-page explanations (conversion and floor mechanics, the stewardship "what it cannot do" list, Fee Router routing, the Recovery Mode interaction) so each concept is defined once and referenced elsewhere.

---

## Polaris Core Concepts

URL: https://docs.polarisfinance.io/core-concepts

# Polaris Core Concepts

This page is the map. Each term is defined in a sentence or two and links to its full treatment. If you read nothing else, read this.

## The assets

- **pAsset**: a synthetic debt asset minted from a Polaris CDP (pUSD, pGOLD, pCHF, …). Each is overcollateralized by pETH and counterparty-free. See [The pAsset Catalog](/minting/passet-catalog).
- **pUSD**: the flagship pAsset, pegged to the US dollar, backed by pETH collateral. See [Minting pAssets](/minting/minting-passets).
- **pETH**: ETH deposited into a two-way [bonding curve](/peth/bonding-curve). It is not CDP-minted debt; it is the *only* collateral Polaris accepts, has a floor price that is designed to rise under the curve invariants, and is yield-bearing.
- **POLAR**: the protocol's stewardship token. After a fixed distribution at launch, new POLAR can be minted *only* by burning pETH in a [conversion auction](/polar/conversion-auctions). **vePOLAR**, a vote-escrow layer for stewardship rights, is forthcoming.

## Minting & positions

- **CDP** (Collateralized Debt Position): the mechanism behind minting. You post collateral and mint the pAsset directly from the protocol, not by borrowing another user's funds. See [Minting pAssets](/minting/minting-passets).
- **Trove**: your individual borrowing position in a Polaris CDP. Each trove is isolated, with its own collateral, debt, and collateralization ratio. See [Managing Your Trove](/minting/managing-your-trove).
- **StablecoinOS**: Polaris is a *factory* of CDP instances, each issuing a different pAsset but all sharing the same pETH collateral and bonding curve.

## Keeping the peg

- **Redemption**: exchanging a pAsset for its underlying collateral at face value (minus a fee) when it trades *below* peg. Restores the floor. See [Redemptions](/redemptions-liquidations/redemptions).
- **Minting (mechanism)**: the inverse of redemption. Arbitrageurs add pETH to troves to mint pAssets when the pAsset trades *above* peg. Restores the ceiling.
- **Autonomous interest rates**: redemption and minting volume serve as onchain proxy signals for the peg, letting Polaris set rates without an external oracle.

## Risk states

- **Liquidation**: if a trove falls below its minimum collateralization ratio, it is liquidated, and the [Stability Pool](/yield/stability-pool) absorbs the debt. See [Liquidations](/redemptions-liquidations/liquidations).
- **Recovery Mode**: a protocol-wide protective state entered when the system-wide reserve-backing-to-debt ratio (the [TCR](/resources/glossary)) falls below a fixed threshold. See [Recovery Mode](/redemptions-liquidations/recovery-mode).
- **Floor price / floor ratio**: the mathematically enforced minimum price of pETH (in ETH), and that floor as a percentage of market price (your maximum drawdown). See [The Floor Price](/peth/floor-price).

## Yield & coordination

- **Stability Pool**: a reserve of pAssets that processes liquidations and is the primary venue for earning yield. See [The Stability Pool](/yield/stability-pool).
- **Conversion auction**: the mechanism that mints POLAR by burning pETH; the most protocol-synergetic action possible. See [Conversion Auctions](/polar/conversion-auctions).
- **Fee Router**: receives pETH from conversions and bonding-curve swap fees, then buffers and distributes it as yield to troves, pro-rata to their debt. See [The Fee Router](/stewardship/fee-router).
- **Stewardship**: a stewardship role steers *quantitative parameters within the bounds the contracts define*. It does not govern the immutable core. See [Stewardship, not Governance](/stewardship/stewardship).

## The shape of the system

A single primitive, the bonding curve, produces pETH. pETH collateralizes every pAsset. Activity on any pAsset generates fees; fees burn pETH and raise its floor; a rising floor strengthens every position. POLAR captures the flywheel's long-term value and steers it.

![Polaris system overview: ETH swaps into pETH on the bonding curve, pETH collateralizes CDPs that mint pUSD, and burning pETH for POLAR raises the floor and releases ETH](/polaris-system-v2.svg)

*The Polaris system at a glance, end to end.*

The diagram traces four steps and an outcome:

1. **Swap.** ETH enters the **bonding curve** and is issued as **pETH**; the curve captures volatility.
2. **Collateral.** pETH is deposited as the sole collateral for **CDPs**.
3. **Borrow.** A CDP mints **pUSD** (or another pAsset) against that pETH, for scalable onchain yield.
4. **Stewardship.** Burning pETH in a conversion auction mints **POLAR**, raising the pETH floor price and releasing ETH.

> Adoption ⇒ activity ⇒ velocity ⇒ fees ⇒ yield ⇒ more demand.

Ready to use it? Start with [Getting Started](/getting-started), or jump to [Minting pAssets](/minting/minting-passets).

---

## Getting Started with Polaris

URL: https://docs.polarisfinance.io/getting-started

# Getting Started with Polaris

The shortest path from "I have ETH" to "I am using Polaris." Each step links to its full treatment.

> **Current status:** Polaris is in public Testnet 2 on Sepolia (`11155111`). Production Ethereum mainnet (`1`) is forthcoming. Check [Polaris Launch Status](/launch-status) before connecting a wallet or treating any page as mainnet-actionable.

## Choose your path

> Building on Polaris? Jump to the [Develop](/paths/develop) path. Just using Polaris? Stay here and pick a journey below.

If you already know why you're here, start with the matching journey:

- **Borrow pAssets** — get pETH, open a trove, mint a pAsset, and manage liquidation risk: [Borrow pAssets](/paths/borrow-passets).
- **Earn yield** — deposit pAssets into a Stability Pool or understand Fee Router yield: [Earn Yield](/paths/earn-yield).
- **Hold or use pETH** — understand the bonding curve, floor price, and external collateral use: [Hold or Use pETH](/paths/hold-use-peth).
- **Convert and lock POLAR** — burn pETH to mint POLAR, lock as vePOLAR, and participate in bounded stewardship: [Convert and Lock POLAR](/paths/convert-lock-polar).
- **Develop** — plan an integration, indexer, vault, analytics surface, or risk tool: [Develop](/paths/develop).
- **Launch status and safety** — confirm official app, network, contracts, audits, and launch status before interacting: [Polaris Launch Status](/launch-status) and [Safety and Verification](/paths/safety-verification).

## What you need

- **An Ethereum wallet** — any standard EOA or smart-account wallet.
- **For Testnet 2:** Sepolia ETH and any test assets required by the current deployment manifest.
- **For production later:** ETH on Ethereum mainnet — the only asset you need to begin; Polaris converts it to [pETH](/peth/bonding-curve) for you.

That's the whole list. No KYC, no whitelist, no offchain account.

## The basic flow

In one line: **deposit ETH ⇒ receive pETH ⇒ open a trove and mint a pAsset ⇒ optionally deposit to the Stability Pool to earn yield.** On Testnet 2 this uses Sepolia test assets; in production it uses Ethereum mainnet once launch status says mainnet is live. Step by step:

1. **Deposit ETH.** Connect your wallet and deposit ETH into the protocol.
2. **Receive pETH from the [bonding curve](/peth/bonding-curve).** Your ETH is swapped for pETH at the curve's current price. pETH is an ETH proxy with a floor price that only rises, and the only collateral Polaris accepts.
3. **Open a [trove](/minting/open-a-trove) and mint a pAsset.** Post your pETH as collateral, then choose which pAsset to mint (pUSD, pGOLD, …) and how much. The pAsset lands in your wallet; the debt stays in your trove. See [the catalog](/minting/passet-catalog) for what you can mint.
4. **Optionally, earn yield.** Deposit your pAsset into the [Stability Pool](/yield/stability-pool) to earn a share of borrower interest plus discounted pETH from liquidations. This is optional — you can instead hold, LP, or use your pAsset anywhere in DeFi.

## Where the app lives

> **Note:** The Polaris app URL is *forthcoming* and will be published on [Polaris Launch Status](/launch-status) before production use. Until then, treat any "Polaris" frontend as unverified.

## Launch verification

Before you connect a wallet or sign a transaction, verify:

- The app URL is linked from this documentation or another official Polaris channel.
- The app is connected to the intended network: Sepolia (`11155111`) for Testnet 2, or Ethereum mainnet (`1`) only after production launch.
- Contract addresses match [Contracts and Addresses](/resources/contracts).
- Verified source, ABIs, and audit status match [Contracts and Addresses](/resources/contracts) and [Audits and Security](/resources/audits-security).
- The product page you're following has been updated from conceptual flow to launch parameters.

## Where to go next

- New to the model? Read [Core Concepts](/core-concepts) for the one-page map, or [Minting pAssets](/minting/minting-passets) for how the CDP works.
- Ready to mint? Go straight to [Open a Trove](/minting/open-a-trove).
- Want to understand the collateral? Start with [The Bonding Curve](/peth/bonding-curve) and [The Floor Price](/peth/floor-price).
- Here for the yield? See [The Stability Pool](/yield/stability-pool).

## Polaris Launch Status

URL: https://docs.polarisfinance.io/launch-status

# Polaris Launch Status

This page is the source of truth for whether Polaris docs are conceptual, testnet-actionable, or production-actionable.

> **Last verified:** June 8, 2026. Polaris is in **public Testnet 2**. Production mainnet is forthcoming.

## Current status

Item | Current status

Public phase | **Testnet 2**
Testnet network | **Sepolia**, chain ID `11155111`
Production network | **Ethereum mainnet**, chain ID `1`, forthcoming
Official app URL | Forthcoming in these docs before production use
Canonical production contracts | Not published yet
Testnet contracts | Available only from the protocol repository deployment manifests; not canonical production addresses
Final audit reports | Not published in these docs yet
Bug bounty / disclosure program | Not public yet

## What is actionable now

Action | Status | Use this rule

Read the protocol model | Conceptual now | The explainers, core concepts, and risk pages are safe to read as the intended model.
Test on Testnet 2 | Testnet-actionable now | Use Sepolia test assets and the protocol repository manifests. Treat addresses, mocks, and parameters as replaceable.
Open testnet app | Testnet-actionable now | Use the official testnet interface at [app.testnet.polarisfinance.io](https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/).
Use production capital | Not yet mainnet-actionable | Wait until this page links the official app, production contracts, verified source, final parameters, and audit status.
Integrate contracts | Plan now, bind later | Build verification gates and schemas now. Do not hardcode unpublished production addresses, ABIs, events, or parameters.

## Network truth

Testnet 2 runs on **Sepolia (`11155111`)** and may use mock assets, mock oracles, redeployable contracts, and testnet-only parameters. It is for testing behavior, not for valuing production risk.

Production Polaris is intended for **Ethereum mainnet (`1`)**, but mainnet app and contract addresses are not published yet. Any frontend, token, contract address, airdrop, support message, or bounty page claiming production status before it is linked here should be treated as unverified.

## Before any transaction

Do not connect a wallet or sign a transaction unless all relevant checks pass:

- The app URL is linked from this page or another official Polaris page.
- The wallet network matches the intended environment: Sepolia for Testnet 2, Ethereum mainnet only after production launch.
- The contract address appears in [Contracts & Addresses](/resources/contracts) or, for testnet-only work, in the official deployment manifest you are using.
- Explorer source and ABI match the documented contract.
- The relevant product page has final values for the parameters your action depends on.
- Audit and disclosure status are clear on [Audits & Security](/resources/audits-security).

## Page labels used in these docs

- **Conceptual only** means the page explains the model and risks, but does not give enough live parameters to sign safely.
- **Testnet-actionable** means you can try the flow on Sepolia with test assets and testnet manifests.
- **Mainnet-actionable later** means the page needs production app links, verified addresses, final parameters, and audit status before use with real value.

For phishing and user-safety checks, continue to [Safety and Verification](/paths/safety-verification). For the intended deployment shape, see [Contracts & Addresses](/resources/contracts).

---

## Why Polaris Finance

URL: https://docs.polarisfinance.io/why-polaris

# Why Polaris Finance

## The trilemma

Stablecoins are said to be **scalable, yield-bearing, or trustless — pick two**. Market structure mostly reinforces that framing: the largest stablecoins rely on issuers, custodians, offchain assets, or other counterparties, while cleaner trustless designs stay smaller.

That trade-off is real, but it is not a law of nature. It exists because, for years, high offchain interest rates made it *easy* to back a stablecoin with T-bills and CEX yield and bring that yield onchain. Easy, but not trustless — and not innovative.

## The degradation pattern

The deeper problem is **structural drift toward centralization**. Stablecoins tend to start relatively clean and get dirtier in how they source yield as they grow, because centralized backing is the path of least resistance to scale.

Stablecoin | Where it falls short

**USDC** | Centralized issuer with freeze/blacklist powers; holders do not receive the issuer's reserve yield directly; stress events have shown banking dependencies matter.
**USDS / DAI (Sky)** | Began with cleaner crypto-collateral roots, then added centralized stablecoin and real-world-asset exposure as it scaled. Governance-set rates remain.
**USDe** | Uses market-neutral, custody/counterparty-dependent infrastructure; scaling can require more counterparties, venues, and operational trust assumptions.
**LUSD / BOLD** | Genuinely trustless and counterparty-free — but limited yield sources and capital inefficiency keep them in a trustless-focused niche. Great to hold, less attractive to mint.

The lesson: a stablecoin built for trustlessness must be *architected* so degradation cannot happen, not merely intend to stay clean.

## The counterparty-free lineage

Polaris descends from the line of immutable, counterparty-free CDP stablecoins, built to fix the limitation each prior generation hit.

![Polaris lineage: SAI (2017) ⇒ LUSD (2021) ⇒ BOLD / Liquity V2 (2024) ⇒ pUSD (2026)](/infographics/polaris-lineage.svg)

*The counterparty-free CDP lineage, 2017 to present.*

In text, the lineage reads:

1. **2017 — SAI:** the first CDP stablecoin, single-collateral. It later upgraded to DAI and then drifted toward centralized backing.
2. **2021 — LUSD:** immutable and decentralized, but capital-inefficient, and spent roughly 20% of its life more than 1% above peg.
3. **2024 — BOLD (Liquity V2):** a refined CDP mechanism from the original Liquity developers — still trustless, still yield-constrained.
4. **2026 — pUSD:** the next chapter. A bonding-curve collateral design that solves the overpeg problem and unlocks onchain-scaling yield without adding a counterparty.

## How Polaris resolves it

Polaris is built so the only counterparty you trust is **immutable code and Ethereum**:

- **Onchain-only yield.** Every yield source — borrower interest, bonding curve swap fees, conversion gains, PolarEX swap fees — is internal to the protocol and grows with activity. Nothing is rented from a CEX or T-bill desk.
- **Immutable core.** The bonding curve, CDP logic, liquidations, redemptions, and the Stability Pool are intended to be fixed in audited deployment code. No admin key can add a custodial PSM, external-collateral module, or freeze function under pressure.
- **Counterparty-free by construction.** pUSD sits in the same counterparty-free lineage as LUSD and BOLD — but adds yield that *scales* with DeFi volume rather than capping growth at what a counterparty can deliver.

> Having a counterparty means capping your growth to whatever yield that counterparty can deliver.

Polaris turns onchain trading volume itself into a stablecoin yield source through its bonding curve, and extends the model to *any* asset through the [StablecoinOS](/core-concepts). DeFi has had uncensorable-but-small and censorable-but-scalable stablecoins. Polaris is built to be the uncensorable, scalable one.

---

[Why Polaris?](https://polarisfinance.io/blog/why-polaris)
Analyzing where USDC, USDS, USDe, and LUSD fall short — and how pUSD delivers scalable, yield-bearing stability without counterparty risk.

---
